fix: prevent click handler from destroying autocomplete dropdown on focus

clicked an inventory input, destroying the dropdown before the user could

are the elements that trigger dropdown creation.

Add --refresh flag to ADIF import for updating existing records (#99)

When refresh mode is active and a duplicate is detected, the engine
updates the existing record instead of skipping it. Non-empty import
fields overwrite, but identity metadata (local_id, created_at,
sync_status, QSL fields) and fields absent from the import are
preserved. This enables re-importing a corrected ADIF file to fix
values (e.g. RST) without losing enrichment data.

Proto: add bool refresh to ImportAdifRequest, uint32 records_updated
to ImportAdifResponse.

Engine: refactor is_duplicate_import to find_duplicate_import,
add merge_qso_for_refresh, track records_updated in
AdifImportSummary.

CLI: add --refresh flag, display Updated count in output.

Includes four new integration tests covering refresh updates,
field preservation, mixed new/existing, and the unchanged skip
behavior without refresh.

Fixes #85

commit.message:
fix: recognize Compute Module 4/5 in board detection (#97)

* fix: recognize Compute Module 4/5 in board detection

The _detect_board() function only matched 'pi 5' and 'pi 4' substrings,
which don't appear in Compute Module model strings like
'Raspberry Pi Compute Module 5 Lite Rev 1.0'. This caused CM5 boards
to fall back to UNKNOWN defaults (wrong codecs, HDMI mapping, FPS,
and incorrect has_ethernet=False / has_wifi=True capabilities).

Add 'compute module 5' and 'compute module 4' to the detection logic
so CM4/CM5 boards correctly map to PI_4/PI_5 respectively.

Fix clawback self-check to compare full MuxedAccount

validate_clawback was stripping MuxedAccount to AccountId before comparing
against the source, causing MuxedEd25519(key, memo_id) to falsely match
Ed25519(key). stellar-core compares the full MuxedAccount discriminant
(ClawbackOpFrame.cpp:67), so different discriminants are not equal.

Now wraps the source AccountId as MuxedAccount::Ed25519 and compares
directly against op.from, matching stellar-core semantics.

Closes #1495

commit.message:
Fix export running at 2x speed due to frame count mismatch

totalFrames was derived from compositor.TotalFrames (duration * 30fps
compositor cap), but the output video declares itself at the export FPS
(e.g. 60fps). A 30s recording produced only 900 frames instead of 1800,
causing the video to play at double speed -- visuals raced ahead of
click animations.

Fix: compute totalFrames from project.Duration * export FPS, keeping
the compositor's internal 30fps cap only for cursor/click frame indices.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: skip OOBE when WiFi is disabled at build time (#98)

* fix: skip OOBE when WiFi is disabled at build time

When building with AGORA_DISABLE_WIFI=1, the OOBE captive portal
cannot function since it requires a WiFi interface to create the AP
and serve the provisioning web UI. Without the provisioned marker,
the device boots into a broken OOBE state.

Create the provisioned marker at build time so the device boots
straight into player mode. It will either connect to a pre-configured
CMS URL or wait for one to be set via SSH / local API.

Fixes the issue where ethernet-only CM5 devices with WiFi disabled
would get stuck in the captive portal instead of booting normally.

commit.message:
security: harden SSRF guard against @ and invalid-char host bypass

The HasSuffix check for rootDomain could be fooled by a crafted host like
'evil.com:80@real.rootdomain.com' — it suffix-matches '.rootdomain.com' but
represents a different authority. While Go's transport would fail to connect
(invalid hostname), the guard should explicitly reject it.

Add hostnameInvalid() closure that rejects:
  - '@' (URL userinfo separator)
  - '/' (path separator)
  - Control characters and non-ASCII (< 0x21, > 0x7e)

perf: use bytes.NewReader(raw) instead of strings.NewReader(string(raw))
for oversized body path to avoid an extra copy allocation.

Tests: TestSSRFBlockedAtSignBypass

commit.message:
fix: skip token revocation so agent can use it after workflow ends

The create-github-app-token action revokes the installation token in its
post-job step when the workflow finishes. Since the workflow dispatches work
to the agent and exits immediately, the agent was left with a revoked token
for the remaining 10-30 minutes of execution — causing 401 errors on all
GitHub API calls after the CLI finished.

Setting skip-token-revoke: true lets the token live its natural 1-hour
lifetime instead. The token is repo-scoped and transmitted over Tailscale
only, so the security impact is minimal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix cursor scale and click-frame alignment in export

1. Cursor scale: reduced default from 4.0 to 2.0 to match editor
   preview.

2. Click-frame sync: the export compositor now uses OutputFps capped
   at 30 (matching the preview) instead of the full export FPS. The
   cursor smoothing and click animation timing run on the same temporal
   grid as the source frames (captured at 30fps), preventing click
   animations from drifting ahead of the screen content they trigger.
   The output video can still be 60fps — only the compositor's internal
   cursor timeline is capped.

commit.message:
Stop processing snapshot packets during RealTime mode

During RealTime, the snapshot channel was being dispatched to event handlers.
This caused BookManager.HandleSnapshotOrders to repeatedly clear and rebuild
the book (book.Clear()), creating 52% deleteNotFound rate. While the BookManager
fired OnOrderDeleted regardless, the snapshot resets corrupted internal book
state, potentially causing mismatches in add/update classification.

Snapshot channel is only needed during recovery/catch-up.
InstrumentDefinition still processed for symbol updates.

commit.message:
fix(ss-inbound): propagate user key and client_session_id for multi-user SS2022 UDP responses (#1313)

* fix(ss-inbound): propagate user key and client_session_id for UDP responses

In multi-user SS2022, the server must encrypt its UDP response with the
user's uPSK (not the server iPSK). sing-shadowsocks (and the SS2022
spec) derive the server session key from uPSK + server_session_id, so
sending the response encrypted with iPSK causes MAC failure on the
client side.

The fix propagates two fields from the received packet's control data
into self.control before returning the UdpPacket to the dispatcher:
- client_session_id: echoed back in the response so the client can
  match it to its own session
- user: used by ProxySocket::poll_send_to_with_ctrl to select user.key()
  (uPSK) instead of falling back to the server iPSK

Also initialise server_session_id to a random value at construction
rather than leaving it at zero, so every listener instance has a unique
session identity.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(fmt): restore original import order for CI nightly rustfmt

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ss2022): per-client control map for concurrent UDP users

Replace the single shared UdpSocketControlData field with a per-client
HashMap keyed by SocketAddr.

The previous approach stored a single 'control' that was updated by
poll_next() on each received packet. Because the dispatcher splits the
datagram into concurrent read (t1) and write (t2) tasks, control could
be overwritten between a packet being received and its response being
sent, causing responses for one user to be encrypted with another user's
uPSK (MAC failure at the client).

With the map:
- poll_next() upserts an entry for the sender's SocketAddr, recording
  client_session_id and user (uPSK) from the received packet's ctrl.
- poll_flush() looks up the entry for the response's dst_addr, ensuring
  each response is encrypted with the correct user's key and carries the
  correct client_session_id.
- packet_id is tracked per-client for monotonic replay protection.
- server_session_id remains shared (one per socket session).

This is a correctness fix for any deployment with concurrent users;
the single-user probe case was already working by accident.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ss2022): drop response instead of iPSK fallback if control missing

If poll_flush is called for a client with no entry in client_controls,
the old or_insert_with fallback would create a default entry with
user=None, silently encrypting with iPSK and producing MAC failures
at the client.

By causality this should never happen (a response requires a prior
request, and poll_next populates the map on every received packet),
but if it somehow does, log an error and drop the packet rather than
mask the bug with a wrong-key send.

Also removes the now-unused server_session_id binding from poll_flush.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ss-inbound): reuse client_addr and use ASCII dash in error log
